title: Kindergarten ELA&L curriculum focus
summary: Foundational listening, speaking, early print awareness, and creative expression.

focus_items.text: Develop listening and speaking skills by sharing ideas, stories, and poems.
Recognize and write some letters and words.
Share understandings of ideas and information about people, places, or things that are real or imaginary.
Express ideas and information creatively.

source_title: English Language Arts and Literature Subject Overview

title: Grade 1 ELA&L curriculum focus
summary: Oral communication, story retell, phonics, and complete-sentence writing.

focus_items.text: Speak about experiences and information using appropriate vocabulary, volume, and speed.
Retell the main idea, characters, and details in a story or poem.
Show understandings of letter-sound relationships and automatic recognition of words.
Combine ideas in logical sequences to speak and write in complete sentences.

source_title: English Language Arts and Literature Subject Overview

title: Grade 2 ELA&L curriculum focus
summary: Clear communication, reading strategies, questioning, and stronger written clarity.

focus_items.text: Adjust listening and speaking to communicate clearly and to develop positive relationships.
Make connections between letters and the sounds they represent to read fluently and spell words correctly.
Use a variety of reading strategies, including altering speed, rereading, and making predictions, to understand the message in a story or an informational text.
Ask and answer questions to clarify information.

source_title: English Language Arts and Literature Subject Overview

title: Grade 3 ELA&L curriculum focus
summary: Oral tradition, expressive fluency, source-inspired writing, and text variety.

focus_items.text: Explore how oral traditions show respect for traditional shared knowledge.
Use eye contact, posture, gestures, and movements to enhance communication.
Read complex words, phrases, and passages fluently and with expression.
Apply writing strategies to create texts that draw upon a variety of sources of inspiration.

source_title: English Language Arts and Literature Subject Overview

title: Grade 4 ELA&L curriculum focus
summary: Relationship-building dialogue, expressive reading, comprehension, and varied text creation.

focus_items.text: Demonstrate listening and speaking that build relationships and understanding.
Demonstrate appropriate emphasis on words, pausing, phrasing, and intonation that reflect comprehension of text.
Examine and apply strategies that support text comprehension.
Create a variety of written texts to share information and develop personal expression.

source_title: English Language Arts and Literature Subject Overview

title: Grade 5 ELA&L curriculum focus
summary: Collaborative dialogue, vocabulary, evaluation, and precise writing for audience and purpose.

focus_items.text: Engage in collaborative dialogue to share ideas, solve problems, and make decisions.
Apply knowledge of vocabulary to reading and writing.
Evaluate ideas and information to comprehend texts.
Analyze and reflect on ways to write effectively for different audiences and purposes.

source_title: English Language Arts and Literature Subject Overview

title: Grade 6 ELA&L curriculum focus
summary: Reasoned dialogue, contextual text analysis, refined writing, and text influence.

focus_items.text: Offer relevant information and logical reasoning to enhance collaborative dialogue.
Analyze texts and interpret contexts to build comprehension.
Refine and adjust writing through creative and critical thinking to reflect individuality and skills.
Analyze how a variety of texts are organized and can influence understandings of ourselves, other people, and the world.

source_title: English Language Arts and Literature Subject Overview

title: Kindergarten mathematics curriculum focus
summary: Counting, shape recognition, measurement comparison, and ordering events in time.

focus_items.text: Count and represent quantities within 10.
Recognize 2-D and 3-D shapes in the environment.
Compare objects by length, area, mass, and capacity.
Order a sequence of events according to time.

title: Grade 1 mathematics curriculum focus
summary: Basic facts, shapes, measurement ordering, and cycles of time.

focus_items.text: Recall addition number facts to a sum of 20 and related subtraction facts.
Identify and sort 2-D and 3-D shapes.
Order objects according to length, area, and capacity.
Identify cycles of time from nature and calendars.

title: Grade 2 mathematics curriculum focus
summary: Addition and subtraction within 100, sorting rules, centimetre measurement, and graphing data.

focus_items.text: Add and subtract numbers within 100.
Sort shapes and describe the sorting rule.
Measure length in centimetres.
Collect and graph data.

title: Grade 3 mathematics curriculum focus
summary: Standard algorithms within 1000, multiplication facts, shape attributes, and telling time.

focus_items.text: Add and subtract numbers within 1000 using standard procedures (algorithms).
Recall multiplication number facts to 10 x 10 and related division facts.
Recognize parallel, perpendicular, and equal sides in 2-D shapes.
Tell time using analog and digital clocks.

title: Grade 4 mathematics curriculum focus
summary: Algorithms within 10 000, multiplication and division, angle-based classification, and graph interpretation.

focus_items.text: Add and subtract numbers within 10 000, including decimal numbers, using standard procedures (algorithms).
Multiply and divide three-digit natural numbers by one-digit natural numbers, using standard procedures (algorithms).
Classify quadrilaterals and triangles using angle and side measurements.
Represent and interpret data in various graphs.

title: Grade 5 mathematics curriculum focus
summary: Large-number algorithms, fraction operations, algebraic expressions, and symmetry.

focus_items.text: Add and subtract numbers within 1 000 000, including decimal numbers, using standard procedures (algorithms).
Multiply three-digit natural numbers by two-digit natural numbers, using standard procedures (algorithms).
Add and subtract fractions with common denominators.
Write and evaluate algebraic expressions.

title: Grade 6 mathematics curriculum focus
summary: Multi-operation problem solving, fraction multiplication, area and volume, and equations.

focus_items.text: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide using standard procedures (algorithms) to solve problems.
Multiply fractions by natural numbers.
Calculate area and volume.
Solve algebraic equations.

title: Kindergarten science curriculum focus
summary: Sensory properties, movement, environmental components, waste reduction, and instructions.

focus_items.text: Explore properties using the five senses.
Examine movement of objects, humans, and other animals.
Examine components of environments.
Protect the environment by reducing waste, recycling, and reusing.

title: Grade 1 science curriculum focus
summary: Measurable properties, movement, seasonal change, organisms, and investigation steps.

focus_items.text: Analyze measurable properties of objects and physical changes.
Investigate characteristics of movement.
Analyze seasonal changes and their effects on plants and animals.
Describe the steps of an investigation and make predictions, observations, and conclusions.

title: Grade 2 science curriculum focus
summary: Materials, light and sound, landforms and water, organism growth, and scientific procedures.

focus_items.text: Examine properties, types, and selection of materials based on suitability, availability, and sustainability.
Investigate the sources and behaviours of light and sound.
Examine Earth's landforms, bodies of water, and relationship to the Sun.
Investigate the growth and development of plants and animals and explore their relationship to humans.

title: Grade 3 science curriculum focus
summary: Water cycle change, contact forces, Earth surface change, fossils, and plant-environment interaction.

focus_items.text: Investigate how substances can change, including water and the water cycle.
Conduct investigations to determine the effects of contact forces on objects, including simple machines.
Analyze changes to Earth's surface caused by natural events and the activities of plants, humans, and other animals, including growing crops and farming.
Analyze interactions between plants, humans, other animals, and the environment.

title: Grade 4 science curriculum focus
summary: Waste management, gravity and magnetism, Earth system interconnections, structures and functions, and evidence.

focus_items.text: Investigate the management of waste materials and describe environmental impacts.
Investigate the non-contact forces of gravity and magnetism.
Analyze the interconnections between Earth's systems (land, air, water, and organisms) and explore conservation activities.
Relate the external structures and sensory organs of organisms to their functions.

title: Grade 5 science curriculum focus
summary: Particle model, forces in fluids, energy resources, climate and agriculture, and experiments.

focus_items.text: Relate the particle model of matter to the states of matter.
Investigate forces in water and air, including buoyant force, lift, and drag.
Examine renewable and non-renewable energy resources.
Analyze the relationships among climate, weather, and agricultural practices.

title: Grade 6 science curriculum focus
summary: Heating and cooling, internal and external forces, energy management, climate change, and ecosystems.

focus_items.text: Relate the heating and cooling of particles to the behaviour of matter, including expansion and contraction.
Analyze internal and external forces and their effects on objects.
Examine scientific, environmental, and economic considerations relating to energy management.
Investigate the characteristics, components, and biodiversity of ecosystems.

title: Kindergarten social studies curriculum focus
summary: Culture, tradition, and community through places, leaders, cooperation, and needs.

focus_items.text: Places in communities.
Cultures, traditions, and histories.
Needs and wants.
Cooperation and collaboration in groups.

title: Grade 1 social studies curriculum focus
summary: Local communities and cultures, physical features, exchange, belonging, and symbols.

focus_items.text: Key physical features and landmarks of communities.
Cultures of diverse communities, including First Nations, Metis, Inuit, and Francophone communities.
Exchange of goods and services.
Supporting belonging and Canada's official symbols.

title: Grade 2 social studies curriculum focus
summary: Canada's communities and heritage through regions, leaders, trade, transportation, and democratic discussion.

focus_items.text: Canada's physical regions and natural resources.
Leaders in Canada, including the prime minister and the premiers.
Traditions and heritages across Canada, including those of First Nations, Metis, Inuit, Francophones, and diverse communities.
Ways trade and transportation support communities.

title: Grade 3 social studies curriculum focus
summary: Alberta and the Prairies through boundaries, governments, contributions, resources, and community participation.

focus_items.text: Physical regions and political boundaries of Alberta.
First Nations and Metis contributions to Alberta and Francophone settlement and contributions to Alberta.
Provincial and municipal governments.
Alberta's natural resources, charitable giving, and volunteerism.

title: Grade 4 social studies curriculum focus
summary: Colonial Canada and Confederation through settlement, transfer of power, rebellion, resources, and citizenship.

focus_items.text: Changes in power and influence in pre-Confederation Canada.
Jacques Cartier and early settlements, Samuel de Champlain, and the establishment of Quebec City and New France.
The Treaty of Paris, the transfer of Canada to the British, the Rebellions of 1837, responsible government, and Confederation.
The fur trade and Canada's natural resources, and citizenship rights and responsibilities.

implementation_notes: Schools may optionally implement new social studies curriculum for grades 4 to 6 in 2025-26. Mandatory implementation begins in 2026-27.

title: Grade 5 social studies curriculum focus
summary: Ancient civilizations through environment, trade, government, legacies, and citizenship.

focus_items.text: The rise and fall of ancient civilizations.
Influence of the environment on ancient civilizations.
Development of economic practices, including trade networks and taxes.
Legacies of ancient civilizations and empires, and informed citizenship.

implementation_notes: Schools may optionally implement new social studies curriculum for grades 4 to 6 in 2025-26. Mandatory implementation begins in 2026-27.

title: Grade 6 social studies curriculum focus
summary: Democracy through principles, historical models, Canadian government, rights, and participation.

focus_items.text: Principles of democracy.
Ancient Athenian direct democracy, Roman Republic representative democracy, and Haudenosaunee Confederacy decision making.
Provincial and federal government in Canada and the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms.
Formal and informal civic participation, discrimination, and racism.

implementation_notes: Schools may optionally implement new social studies curriculum for grades 4 to 6 in 2025-26. Mandatory implementation begins in 2026-27.
